self_correction_logic: Q1. I checked the sample; it consists of IJV CEOs and senior VPs, not students. Q2. The correlation matrix N = 185 IJVs, which represents firms (dyads of IJV and parent firm), not individuals. Q3. The study measures CEO organizational identification and inter-firm cooperation, but does not measure individual boundary spanning behavior. Q4. They measured organizational identification, not mere communication. Q5. BSB does not appear in the correlation matrix at all. Q6. This is not Leader BSB. Q7. This is not Intra-Organizational BSB. Q8. The construct measured is organizational identification (an attitude), not BSB. Therefore, the study lacks a valid BSB measure (Code 1) and is conducted at the firm-level (Code 3). It must be excluded under Code 5 [Code 1 & Code 3].
reason_summary: The study does not measure individual boundary spanning behavior, focusing instead on CEO organizational identification and inter-firm cooperation. Additionally, the unit of analysis is the firm (N = 185 IJVs) rather than individuals, violating the individual-level analysis requirement.
